segments.integrated_caption: A man, later identified as Robin, sits at a wooden desk in a warmly lit, stylishly decorated room, engaging directly with the camera in what appears to be a vlog for his YouTube channel, 'Man For Himself.' He speaks animatedly, using expressive hand gestures—pointing, clasping his hands, and making open-palmed motions—to emphasize his points as he introduces himself and his channel, which focuses on hair product reviews, tutorials, grooming, skincare, and fragrance. Behind him, a computer monitor displays a website with product images related to men’s grooming and lifestyle, specifically Tom Ford fragrances, which Robin confirms he is discussing, noting he has '19 in total' across signature and private blends. On the desk to his left, a vase of colorful flowers, a lit candle, and several dark bottles, explicitly identified as his Tom Ford fragrance collection, are neatly arranged alongside a clear glass mug containing a dark liquid, which he later implies is coffee, part of his coping mechanism. Robin explains his personal motivation for the video, describing it as 'therapy' to cope with feeling 'a bit shit' and 'over this lockdown,' and mentions his strategy of wearing a 'full blue outfit' (referencing his teal henley) and drinking coffee. Mid-segment, a 'SUBSCRIBE NOW!' graphic appears, followed by a split-screen layout showing his social media handle '@MANFORHIMSELF' with links to Instagram, Twitter, and TikTok, alongside a stylized photo of him posing with arms crossed, reinforcing his call to action. The atmosphere remains inviting and professional, with soft lighting and tasteful decor including framed artwork and plants, underscoring the personal brand and lifestyle content focus as Robin invites viewers to get comfortable with a drink.

segments.integrated_caption: The Man in Teal Henley Shirt, with a clear, articulate male voice, opens the segment seated at his desk, holding the small, clear glass TOM FORD BEAU DE JOUR Perfume Bottle. He gestures expressively while speaking to the camera, describing the fragrance with notes of "Basil, patchouli, lavender, geranium," adding, "It's fresh, it's bright, it's masculine-ish, it's wearable, and I like it. I like it a lot." He then unscrews the cap to reveal a White Applicator Stick, demonstrating its use. He compares it with the dark, square-shaped TOM FORD OUD WOOD Perfume Bottle, highlighting design differences. The scene cuts to a close-up of the TOM FORD OUD MINERALE Perfume Bottle on a wooden surface with blurred green plants. The video returns to the man, who now holds the OUD MINERALE bottle and the applicator stick, continuing his explanation with animated hand movements. On-screen text appears, reading '6 VERT DE FLEUR MAN FOR HIMSELF', as he transitions to discussing "Vert de Fleur," stating, "This fragrance, I'm pretty sure, yes, it has. It has been discontinued." He mentions receiving it as a gift and then declares, "Vert de Fleur, out of all of these, is my least favorite." segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with the Man in Teal Henley Shirt seated at his desk, holding a small, rectangular pink and white ROSE PRICK product box. He speaks expressively in a clear, articulate voice, describing a previous product as "sweeter" with "great earthiness," noting it "works so well, especially for us guys." He then announces, "Okay, I'm going to stick with these and I'm going to jump into lavender," as he opens the box to reveal a small, clear glass bottle with a silver cap and a White Applicator Stick. He unscrews the cap, holds the stick up to the camera, and emphatically declares, "Now, Lavender Extreme is one of my favorites!" He demonstrates applying the product to his wrist while explaining that Lavender Extreme "came out on the sort of same sort of limited edition run of that year, the year before." The On-Screen Text Overlay changes to '8 LAVENDER EXTREME MAN FOR HIMSELF', and the camera cuts to a close-up of the TOM FORD LAVENDER EXTREME EAU DE PARFUM 50 ML bottle, a clear, square-shaped glass bottle with a silver cap and label, resting on a wooden surface with blurred green plants in the background. The man returns, holding the bottle and the White Applicator Stick, continuing to explain its features with animated hand gestures. He details the scent's composition, stating it "has like three types of lavender. It's either three or five," and clarifies, "this is lavender, and this is lavender not like you know it." He points to the bottle, emphasizing its design, and gestures toward the camera, engaging the viewer, while contrasting the scent with common expectations, mentioning "Verda Fleur" or "Bodgeur," but asserting, "it's not." He describes it as "so playful," "so lively," with "citrus accents in it," specifically "Italian citrus fruits," not "any old Tesco bought in the UK citrus fruits," and adds, "there's also carrot seed." Throughout, the background remains consistent with the Computer Monitor displaying a website, Desk Decor including a vase of colorful flowers, a lit candle, and dark bottles, and Framed Artwork and Plants, all under warm, soft lighting, enhancing the intimate, personal atmosphere of the review.

It's um, it's the almond." He then adds, "it is beyond fragrant," before standing up and moving out of frame, leaving the desk and its contents in view.
